package router

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for gin_route_paths_repeated_as_raw_strings
// Anti-pattern: Route path literals repeated across multiple registrations instead of using constants or groups.
func SetupRouter() *gin.Engine {
	r := gin.Default()

	// Anti-pattern: "/api/v1/users" repeated as raw string in every registration
	r.GET("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"users": []string{}})
	})
	r.POST("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": 1})
	})
	r.PUT("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"updated": true})
	})
	r.DELETE("/api/v1/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"deleted": true})
	})

	// Anti-pattern: "/api/v1/users/:id" also repeated as raw string
	r.GET("/api/v1/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id")})
	})
	r.PUT("/api/v1/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id"), "updated": true})
	})
	r.DELETE("/api/v1/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id"), "deleted": true})
	})

	return r
}
